name: Montsec Bikepacking Loop 2 - Montsec d'Estall
description: This difficult 26.3-mile touring cycling route, Montsec Bikepacking Loop 2 - Montsec d'Estall, offers 3194 feet of elevation gain in Serra d
region_intro: Touring cycling around Camporrells, located in the province of Huesca, Aragon, Spain, offers varied terrain from rolling hills and agricultural plains to the foothills of the Pyrenees. The region is characterized by quiet roads, providing a tranquil cycling experience. Natural features such as the Baldellou Gorge and the Canelles Reservoir contribute to scenic routes, while nearby historical villages add cultural interest. Best touring cycling routes around Camporrells The most popular touring cycling route is Camporells and Estopiñán del Castillo, a 26.3 miles (42.2 km) difficult trail that takes 3 hours 54 minutes to complete, offering significant elevation gain. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Congosto de Baldellou and Coll de Baldellou passing through Camporells— circular, a difficult 18.0 miles (28.9 km) path that winds through the scenic Baldellou Gorge. Local touring cyclists also love the Penavera Bridge – Congost de Sacs loop from Cantera La Soriana, a 13.1 miles (21.0 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 2 hours 12 minutes. Touring cycling around Camporrells is defined by rolling agricultural landscapes, quiet roads, and views of the pre-Pyrenean foothills. The network offers options for cyclists seeking challenging routes with notable elevation changes. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 70 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Camporrells's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Camporrells?
A: There are over 15 touring cycling routes around Camporrells, offering a diverse range of experiences for cyclists. These routes vary in difficulty and length, providing options for different skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when touring cycling near Camporrells?
A: The terrain around Camporrells is quite varied, ranging from rolling hills and agricultural plains to the foothills of the Pyrenees. You can expect a mix of quiet roads and paths, providing a tranquil cycling experience with diverse landscapes.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the Camporrells area?
A: Yes, several routes are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1360099021/congosto-de-baldellou-and-coll-de-baldellou-passing-through-camporells-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Congosto de Baldellou and Coll de Baldellou passing through Camporells— circular&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 18.0-mile (28.9 km) path that winds through the scenic Baldellou Gorge, offering a rewarding circular journey.

Q: What are some scenic natural attractions I can see along the touring cycling routes?
A: The region boasts several natural highlights. You can cycle past the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2450973" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Baldellou Gorge&lt;/a&gt;, or discover the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2434640" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sosa Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt;. The area also features impressive viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7999235" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Savinós Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, offering stunning vistas of the Canelles Reservoir and Mont-rebei Gorge.

Q: Are there historical or cultural landmarks to explore while cycling?
A: Absolutely. The Huesca province is rich in medieval heritage. You might encounter ancient villages and historical sites. A notable historical point of interest is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4446234" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Finestras Abandoned Village&lt;/a&gt;, which can be part of a longer route like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40311243" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Penavera Bridge – Finestres Abandoned Village loop from Estopiñán del Castillo / Estopanyá&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any family-friendly touring cycling routes around Camporrells?
A: While many routes in the immediate Camporrells area are rated as difficult due to significant elevation changes, the broader Aragon region offers varied terrain. For family-friendly options, it's best to look for routes with less elevation gain and shorter distances. Always check the route details on komoot for specific difficulty ratings and elevation profiles to ensure it suits your family's capabilities.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Camporrells?
A: The Huesca province generally offers pleasant cycling conditions outside of the peak summer heat and potential winter cold. Spring and autumn are often ideal, with milder temperatures and beautiful landscapes. Always check local weather forecasts before heading out.

Q: What do other cyclists say about touring cycling in Camporrells?
A: The komoot community highly rates the touring cycling experience in Camporrells, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Cyclists often praise the quiet roads, the varied topography from agricultural plains to pre-Pyrenean foothills, and the scenic natural features like gorges and reservoirs.

Q: Are there routes that offer significant elevation challenges?
A: Yes, many routes around Camporrells are classified as difficult and feature significant elevation changes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1360085463/camporells-and-estopinan-del-castillo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camporells and Estopiñán del Castillo&lt;/a&gt; route covers 26.3 miles (42.2 km) with nearly 1,000 meters of elevation gain, providing a substantial challenge for experienced touring cyclists.

Q: Where can I find information about parking facilities for cyclists in Camporrells?
A: While specific parking facilities for cyclists in Camporrells are not detailed, the region's small villages typically offer general parking areas. It's advisable to check local village maps or use navigation apps to locate suitable parking near your chosen route's starting point.

Q: Are there any specific permits required for cycling in the Huesca Aragon region?
A: Generally, no specific permits are required for touring cycling on public roads and established trails in the Huesca Aragon region. However, if your route passes through protected natural parks or private land, it's always best to check local regulations or park authority websites for any specific rules or restrictions.

Q: Can I bring my dog on touring cycling routes around Camporrells?
A: While many outdoor areas in Aragon are dog-friendly, specific rules can vary depending on the route and whether it passes through protected natural areas or private land. It's recommended to keep dogs on a leash and ensure they are well-behaved. Always check for signage or local guidelines regarding pets on specific trails.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by water features like reservoirs?
A: Yes, the region is home to beautiful water features. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19886592" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Penavera Bridge – Congost de Sacs loop from Cantera La Soriana&lt;/a&gt;, for example, offers views of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7158840" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Penavera Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, which is near the Canelles Reservoir. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3954955" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canelles Dam&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1132485" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Ana Reservoir and Dam&lt;/a&gt; are also significant water bodies in the vicinity.

Q: How long are the touring cycling routes typically around Camporrells?
A: The touring cycling routes around Camporrells vary in length. You can find routes ranging from moderate distances, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6954884" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Santo Toribio loop from Baélls&lt;/a&gt; at 13.0 miles (20.8 km), to longer, more challenging rides like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1360085463/camporells-and-estopinan-del-castillo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camporells and Estopiñán del Castillo&lt;/a&gt; route, which is 26.3 miles (42.2 km) long.
tips: From this part of the track you can enjoy an incredible view of Montsec d'Ares and Montsec de Rubies. This is the central part of the Montsec mountain range, which stretches between the cliffs of Montrebei in the west and Terradets in the east.
Estopiñán del Castillo has the gigantic Canelles reservoir, the spectacular Santa Ana reservoir and an ophite stone farm that has supplied the AVE between Zaragoza and Tarragona.

faqs.question: Is the Montsec Bikepacking Loop 2 - Montsec d'Estall suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This loop is rated as difficult and requires good physical condition. It features significant elevation gain and a mix of surfaces, including gravel roads and tracks, with some sections potentially requiring you to push your bike. It's best suited for experienced touring cyclists equipped with gravel or mountain bikes.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this bikepacking loop?
faqs.answer: The terrain is mountainous and varied, characterized by numerous climbs and descents. You'll encounter a mix of surfaces, primarily gravel roads and tracks. The route winds through stunning natural landscapes, including limestone peaks, rivers, canyons, and reservoirs.

faqs.question: What natural features and landmarks can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The loop offers breathtaking views and passes by several notable features. You'll enjoy Views of Montsec d'Ares and Montsec de Rubies, and Views of Estopiñán del Castillo. Other highlights in the broader Montsec d'Estall area include the Congosto de Siscar, the Canelles reservoir, the Peñavera bridge, Finestres, Les Roques de la Vila (also known as the "Chinese Wall of Finestres"), and the medieval village of Montañana. The Montsec d'Estall summit itself provides expansive panoramic views.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to ride this bikepacking loop?
faqs.answer: The Montsec region can experience varying water availability, especially in summer. Spring and autumn generally offer more moderate temperatures and better water access, making them ideal for bikepacking. Always check local weather conditions before you go.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Montsec Bikepacking Loop 2 - Montsec d'Estall?
faqs.answer: As a circular route, you can typically start and finish at various points. Specific parking information for the trailhead isn't detailed, but you should look for parking options in nearby villages or designated areas that allow for multi-day vehicle storage, keeping in mind the route's starting point is often Camporrells or Estopiñán del Castillo.

faqs.question: Is this bikepacking route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally allowed on trails in the Montsec region, it's crucial to keep them on a leash to protect local wildlife and livestock. Be prepared to carry enough water for your dog, especially during warmer months, as water sources can be scarce.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay an entrance fee for this area?
faqs.answer: The Montsec region, including the Serra del Montsec, generally does not require specific permits or entrance fees for cycling on public trails. However, always respect private property and any posted regulations. There are no specific fees mentioned for this bikepacking loop.

faqs.question: Are there water sources or amenities available along the trail?
faqs.answer: The route passes through traditional villages where you might find opportunities for local food and to replenish supplies. However, water availability can vary, especially in summer, so it's advisable to carry sufficient water for your journey and plan your stops carefully.

faqs.question: What type of bike and gear do I need for this loop?
faqs.answer: Given the mountainous terrain and mixed surfaces, a gravel bike or a mountain bike is highly recommended. Ensure your bike is equipped for bikepacking, meaning it can carry your gear for multiple days. You'll need appropriate clothing for varying weather, navigation tools, repair kits, and sufficient food and water.

faqs.question: Does this loop connect with other trails in the Montsec region?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several other trails. You'll find sections that overlap with the La Ribagorza. BTT. Ruta 29. Vuelta al Montsec de l'Estall and La Ribagorza. BTT. Ruta 30. Enduro Montsec. It also briefly touches upon the Montsec Bikepacking Loop 1 - La Ribagorza baja and the La Ribagorza. BTT. Ruta 7. Lagunas de Estaña.

faqs.question: How many days should I plan for the Montsec Bikepacking Loop 2 - Montsec d'Estall?
faqs.answer: While the recorded duration for this specific segment is approximately 3.9 hours for touring cycling, the full 'Montsec Bikepacking Loop 2' implies a multi-day adventure. Given its 'difficult' rating and significant elevation, planning for 2-3 days would allow you to fully experience the route, enjoy the scenery, and adhere to the 'cycling without hurry' philosophy of bikepacking.

waypoints.name: Views of Montsec d'Ares and Montsec de Rubies
